title: King Size Homer

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening credits conclude
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy but low originality by this point in series

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: I've never seen a man so desperate to get out of five minutes of calisthenics
jokes.setup: Homer is hiding in bathroom stall to avoid exercise
jokes.punchline: Instructor's deadpan observation about Homer's extreme lengths to avoid minimal exercise
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor with nice specificity about 'five minutes' emphasizing Homer's laziness

dialogue.speaker: Exercise Instructor
dialogue.line: I've seen a lot of people avoid exercise, but hiding in a bathroom stall? That's a new low. And I've been doing this for twenty years.

jokes.text: Especially my big fat mouth
jokes.setup: Lenny says every muscle is getting a workout
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about his own tendency to talk
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting physical exercise to verbal exercise

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Every muscle is getting a workout.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: You know, talking about it is making me thirsty. Let's go to Moe's!

jokes.text: Yeah, especially your big fat- Oh, wait.
jokes.setup: Carl starts to agree and insult Lenny
jokes.punchline: Carl catches himself mid-insult, creating awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction comedy with good timing

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Yeah, Lenny, you're a real—wait, no, you're a good guy.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: ...Thanks?

jokes.text: I want to see more Teddy Roosevelts and less Franklin Roosevelts
jokes.setup: Instructor motivating workers during exercise
jokes.punchline: Clever reference to wheelchair-bound FDR vs. vigorous Teddy Roosevelt
jokes.explanation: Excellent historical reference joke with perfect economy and clever wordplay about energy levels

dialogue.speaker: Exercise Instructor
dialogue.line: Come on, people! You gotta push it! Push it! Remember, FDR was in a wheelchair and he ran this country!

dialogue.speaker: Exercise Instructor
dialogue.line: Teddy Roosevelt? He was out there fighting bears! You can do twenty more sit-ups!

jokes.text: Uh, actually, Homer, that's just one. See, each pushup includes both an up part and a down part.
jokes.setup: Homer proudly announces 'Two!' after minimal effort
jokes.punchline: Instructor patiently explains basic pushup mechanics to grown man
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's complete lack of fitness knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Two!

dialogue.speaker: Instructor
dialogue.line: Sir, you have to go all the way down and all the way back up. That's one pushup.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he misunderstood pushups
jokes.punchline: Classic frustrated Homer exclamation
jokes.explanation: Callback to opening D'oh with perfect character timing

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: It's like a lottery that rewards stupidity
jokes.setup: Discussion about Charlie getting disability pay for work injury
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate observation about disability system abuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and structure - sets up Homer's entire scheme with perfect satirical edge

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got disability pay once. Broke my back lifting a box of donuts at work.

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: A box of donuts? That's nothing.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, the box was on top of my head at the time.

jokes.text: Stupidity, eh? Must hurt self.
jokes.setup: Homer overhears about disability lottery
jokes.punchline: Homer's caveman-like internal monologue planning self-injury
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer voice - primitive grammar showing his simple thought process

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Disability lottery? Why didn't anyone tell me about this? All I have to do is injure myself and I can get money from the government?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Let's see... I could fall down the stairs, or maybe get hit by a car. No, wait—I could stick my hand in the meat grinder at work!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This is the best day of my life. Finally, all my dreams are coming true.

jokes.text: Hmm. Probably better that didn't hit me.
jokes.setup: Homer attempts to hurt himself but fails
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about avoiding injury when trying to get injured
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal of Homer's goal with his natural self-preservation instinct

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why won't anybody hurt me? I'm not even gonna try anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Now that I'm not trying, I'll probably get hit by a car and die.

jokes.text: could you give me a little push in the opposite direction?
jokes.setup: Homer stuck in revolving door
jokes.punchline: Polite request for help with simple mechanical problem
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy with Homer's characteristic politeness in embarrassing situations

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Help! Help! I'm stuck in a revolving door!

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Just step out, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I can't! It won't stop!

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Push the emergency button.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: There's a button?

jokes.text: Carpal tunnel syndrome? No. Lumber lung? No. Juggler's despair? No. Achy-breaky pelvis? No.
jokes.setup: Homer reading through disability conditions
jokes.punchline: Mix of real conditions with absurd fake ones like 'Juggler's despair' and 'Achy-breaky pelvis'
jokes.explanation: Creative fake medical conditions with good wordplay, especially 'Achy-breaky pelvis' riffing on Billy Ray Cyrus

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Let's see... Juggler's despair... Achy-breaky pelvis... Booleans... Chronic Weasel Bite...

jokes.text: Oh! I'm never gonna be disabled. I'm sick of being so healthy.
jokes.setup: Homer frustrated by inability to qualify for disability
jokes.punchline: Complaining about being too healthy while clearly being out of shape
jokes.explanation: Good ironic character moment - Homer's warped self-perception vs. reality

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, it's unfair! I can't get disability because I'm too healthy. Me! Too healthy! Do you know how hard I've worked to stay this way?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I haven't exercised in years, I eat nothing but donuts and beer, and somehow my body just keeps on ticking like some kind of well-oiled machine that runs on grease and regret.

jokes.text: If you weigh more than 300 pounds... you qualify as disabled.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers weight-based disability qualification
jokes.punchline: The realization that sets up the entire episode plot
jokes.explanation: Important plot setup that's funny in context of Homer's desperation

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... if I'm too heavy to work, the government will pay me to stay home?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! I'm gonna be rich!

jokes.text: Dad, I know we don't do a lot together... but helping you gain 61 pounds is something I want to be a part of.
jokes.setup: Bart hearing about Homer's weight gain plan
jokes.punchline: Bart's twisted enthusiasm for enabling father's unhealthy behavior as bonding activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - perfectly captures Bart's mischievous nature and dysfunctional father-son relationship

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, you're gonna get so fat! This is gonna be awesome!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: We can go to all-you-can-eat buffets together, and I'll encourage you to eat more and more until you can barely move!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's my boy!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: This is the best father-son bonding ever.

jokes.text: All my life, I've been an obese man trapped inside a fat man's body.
jokes.setup: Homer justifying his weight gain plan
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'thin person trapped in fat body' cliche with absurd escalation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody line with perfect structure and economy - became instantly quotable and culturally referenced

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not fat, I'm just a thin person trapped in a fat body. A thin person trapped in a fat body trapped in a bigger fat body. And that fat body is trapped in an even BIGGER fat body!

jokes.text: If you want to add to her worries, go ahead. I guess I'm just a little more grateful for all she's done for us.
jokes.setup: Lisa threatens to tell Marge about the plan
jokes.punchline: Homer guilt-tripping Lisa with fake moral superiority
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's manipulative side

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm going to tell Mom about your plan.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Lisa, Lisa, Lisa... I'm disappointed in you. When I was your age, I would never have betrayed a family member's trust like this. I had integrity. I had honor. I had... well, I had a job at the nuclear plant, but the point is, you're breaking my heart here.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, you once sold my saxophone to buy beer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That was a different time, sweetie. A different Homer.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, we'll just see about that, little Miss Smart Guy.
jokes.setup: Lisa warns about health risks of obesity
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive response with confused gendered insult
jokes.explanation: Good Homer-ism with the gender confusion in 'little Miss Smart Guy'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, shut up, Lisa! You know, I'm not obese, I'm just big-boned. And for your information, I'm not confused about genders — you're the one who's a girl!

jokes.text: My God! That's monstrous. I've never heard of anything so negligen- I'll have no part of it. Can you recommend a doctor who will?
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert morally objects to Homer's plan
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal to helpfully referring Homer to unethical doctor
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection comedy with perfect character beat - Hibbert's professional ethics vs. helpful nature

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: I can't in good conscience help you with that, Homer. It's unethical and potentially dangerous.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: But if you really need it done, I know a guy. Dr. Nick Riviera. He'll do anything for money.

jokes.text: Hi, everybody! Hi, Dr. Nick!
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick enters scene
jokes.punchline: His signature greeting exchange with patients
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick running gag - perfect character integration but low originality

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Hello, everybody!

dialogue.speaker: Patients
dialogue.line: Hello, Dr. Nick!

jokes.text: assal horizontology
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick prescribing treatment methods
jokes.punchline: Fake medical term for lying down/being horizontal
jokes.explanation: Clever fake medical terminology that sounds plausible from quack doctor

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: You're gonna want to lie down for this one.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: I'm prescribing a nice case of horizontalitis.

jokes.text: the neglected food groups... such as the whipped group, the congealed group... and the chocotastic
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick discussing nutrition
jokes.punchline: Completely unscientific food groups based on texture and made-up words
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist nutrition advice with memorable made-up word 'chocotastic'

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: Now, let me tell you about the four basic food groups: greasy, crunchy, chewy, and gooey. You see, the human body is 60% water, 40% hot air, and 100% gristle.

jokes.text: Instead of making sandwiches with bread, use Pop-Tarts. Instead of chewing gum, chew bacon.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick giving weight gain advice
jokes.punchline: Absurdly unhealthy substitutions that escalate in ridiculousness
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity with memorable specific examples

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: Now, if you want to gain weight, you can't just eat any food. You need to eat foods that are high in calories and low in nutrition. For example, instead of regular soda, try mixing soda with mayonnaise. Instead of eating one bucket of lard, eat two buckets of lard. And instead of going to the gym, go to a chewing gum factory and inhale the fumes. You'll gain weight in no time!

jokes.text: You could brush your teeth with milk shakes.
jokes.setup: Continuation of unhealthy substitution advice
jokes.punchline: Peak absurdity - replacing hygiene with junk food
jokes.explanation: Good peak to the escalation of absurd health advice

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Instead of washing your hands, just eat more cupcakes. The sugar kills the germs!

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.line: That doesn't sound right...

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: I'm a doctor!

jokes.text: Hey, did you go to Hollywood Upstairs Medical College too?
jokes.setup: Homer impressed by Dr. Nick's 'medical advice'
jokes.punchline: Homer recognizing terrible advice but thinking it's from same 'school' as his knowledge
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with great fake institution name that became quotable

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, Dr. Nick, you're the first doctor to ever make sense to me.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Well, thank you, Homer. That's very kind of you to say.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, we must have gone to the same school.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Well, I went to medical school in the Caribbean.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I went to a school in the Caribbean too! It was called 'The School of Hard Knocks,' and boy, did I learn a lot!

jokes.text: rub it against a piece of paper. If the paper turns clear, it's your window to weight gain.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick giving final advice about food selection
jokes.punchline: Completely unscientific grease test presented as medical wisdom
jokes.explanation: Memorably absurd pseudo-scientific test with good visual component

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Now, to check if food is too greasy, hold it up to the light. If you can see through it, it's fine. If you can't see through it, it's too greasy.

jokes.text: Eat around the banana, Dad. It's just empty vitamins.
jokes.setup: Bart helping Homer with his eating binge
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards health advice treating vitamins as worthless
jokes.explanation: Perfect inversion of health advice with great phrase 'empty vitamins' - shows Bart's commitment to enabling dad

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey dad, if you're gonna eat all that junk, you should at least take some vitamins.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Vitamins? Bah! They're just a scam by the health food industry to make you feel guilty about eating real food.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But dad, vitamins have important nutrients that—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Nutrients, schmutrients! The only nutrients I need come from donuts, beer, and pork chops. That's a balanced diet right there.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Okay, but what about stuff like vitamin C for your immune system?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Immune system? That's just something doctors made up so they could sell more pills. I've never needed vitamins and I've never been healthier!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, you just said you've been sick for three weeks straight.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly! Proof that vitamins don't work. Now hand me that box of donuts.

jokes.text: Lucky for you, this stuff doesn't work.
jokes.setup: Homer about to eat exercise powder
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual dismissal of health product while enabling unhealthy behavior
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Bart's cynical view of health products

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey Dad, that's not a donut.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: It's exercise powder.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmmm... exercise powder.

jokes.text: Homer. You're, uh, on the towel rack.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks he's lost weight on the scale
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal that Homer is too heavy for scale and standing on towel rack
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag with perfect deadpan delivery from Bart

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! I've lost three pounds!

jokes.text: When's your next coffee break? Anytime I want.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about Homer's work schedule
jokes.punchline: Homer's smug satisfaction with complete work flexibility
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's satisfaction with his scheme

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, what time do you have to be at work tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Work? Tomorrow? Pfft, I don't have to be there until 9.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But isn't it 9:15 right now?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly. I'm already 15 minutes late. When you're as important as me, they just let it slide.

jokes.text: A crazy guy shot a bunch of people, and a subway ran over my hat.
jokes.setup: Flanders describing his bad day
jokes.punchline: Escalation from serious violence to trivial hat loss presented equally
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with dark comedy and perfect anticlimax

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, it started when I was hit by a car, then I was mugged, then my house burned down.

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: But worst of all, I lost my hat.

jokes.text: Oh. So that's it, isn't it, Marge? Looks. I didn't know you were so shallow.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing concern about Homer's appearance and health
jokes.punchline: Homer deflecting legitimate health concerns as vanity
jokes.explanation: Good character defense mechanism with typical Homer deflection

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you need to see a doctor. You're getting out of breath just walking up the stairs.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, I'm fine. Besides, doctors just want to make you feel bad about yourself. Last time Doc Hibbert saw me, he said I had high cholesterol, high blood pressure, and that my liver looked like a pâté.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's serious, Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It's just vanity, Marge. He's trying to sell me on some diet nonsense so I'll look like those pretty boys on TV.

jokes.text: I would love you if you weighed 1,000 pounds. But-
jokes.setup: Marge trying to reassure Homer about unconditional love
jokes.punchline: Absurd weight exaggeration followed by inevitable 'but'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and typical relationship comedy with 'but' undercutting the reassurance

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, I love you no matter what. Even if you weighed 500 pounds...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Even if you weighed 1000 pounds...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Even if you weighed 2000 pounds and couldn't fit through the door...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm listening...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: But you'd have to stop eating donuts.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Dad, it says 'Non-Toxic.' Well, that's a plus.
jokes.setup: Homer about to eat wax donut
jokes.punchline: Homer finding minimal positive in eating non-food item
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with Homer's twisted optimism about eating wax

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... wax donut.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, at least it's not as bad as the time I ate that candle.

jokes.text: Uh, Dad. Towel rack.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating on scale again
jokes.punchline: Repeat of towel rack gag
jokes.explanation: Effective callback with even more economy - just two words needed

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! I'm down to 239 pounds!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's wonderful, Homie!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm so happy, I'm gonna go hang this scale on the wall!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, no! That's a towel rack!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: What's the name of this gastropod?
jokes.setup: Burns asking about Homer
jokes.punchline: Burns using scientific term for slug to describe fat Homer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns vocabulary with scientific precision for insult

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: What's wrong with Simpson? He's as slow as a gastropod.

jokes.text: What's the connection? Must be the nonstop sitting and snacking.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning computer worker stereotype
jokes.punchline: Homer accurately identifying the lifestyle connection
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with rare Homer self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Computer guys are always going on about the same thing. They say 'We're tired. We work too hard. Our backs hurt. Our wrists hurt.' But you know what? If you didn't spend so much time sitting in front of a computer, you wouldn't have these problems!

jokes.text: Ponchos, muumuus, capes, jumpsuits... unisheets, muslin body rolls, academic and judicial robes.
jokes.setup: Clerk listing clothing alternatives for large people
jokes.punchline: Escalating from practical to absurd to dignified options
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with mix of real and absurd options, especially 'muslin body rolls'

dialogue.speaker: Clothing Store Clerk
dialogue.line: Well, for a man of your... stature, we have these relaxed-fit jeans. Or these cargo pants with the expandable waistband. We also carry muumuus, dashikis, and a lovely caftan collection. And of course, we have these elegant togas and cloaks for when you want to feel dignified.

jokes.text: I don't wanna look like a weirdo. I'll just go with a muumuu.
jokes.setup: Homer rejecting weird-sounding options
jokes.punchline: Choosing traditionally feminine garment to avoid looking weird
jokes.explanation: Good ironic choice with Homer's obliviousness to social norms

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No way! A dress? That's for girls!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait... nobody would think that's weird?

jokes.text: Hee-hee-hee! I pity those poor suckers on the freeway. Gas, brake, honk. Gas, brake, honk. Honk, honk, punch. Gas, gas, gas.
jokes.setup: Homer watching traffic report from home
jokes.punchline: Homer's smugness followed by reporter's escalating road rage description
jokes.explanation: Good escalating road rage sequence with Homer's schadenfreude

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Heh heh, I'm glad I'm not stuck in traffic.

dialogue.speaker: News Reporter
dialogue.line: We're reporting bumper-to-bumper traffic on the interstate. Drivers are honking, swearing, and making obscene gestures.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Suckers.

dialogue.speaker: News Reporter
dialogue.line: One driver has actually abandoned his car and is now chasing another motorist down the shoulder with a tire iron!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: except for all those daylight savings days. Lousy farmers.
jokes.setup: Homer noting he's early to work for first time
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming farmers for daylight saving time confusion
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic with misdirected blame about daylight saving time

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm early to work for the first time ever!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Those darn farmers and their daylight saving time!

jokes.text: To start, press any key. Where's the 'Any' key? I see 'Esk,' 'Catarol,' and 'Pig Up.' There doesn't seem to be any 'Any' key.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to start computer
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of 'any key' plus misreading keyboard keys
jokes.explanation: Classic computer illiteracy joke with perfect Homer logic and became widely quoted

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It says 'Press any key to continue.' Where's the any key?

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: What are you looking for?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It says I need to press any key. All I see here is asterisk, ampersand, and, uh... (squinting) 'esc', 'tab', 'enter'... It says 'any key'!

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Well, what do you do?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm going to lean on the 'any' key.

jokes.text: All this computer hacking is making me thirsty. I think I'll order a Tab.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with basic computer operation
jokes.punchline: Homer calling basic computer use 'hacking' and wanting Tab soda
jokes.explanation: Good character humor with Homer's tech terminology confusion

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm in! I'm hacking into the mainframe!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! Now get me a Tab.

jokes.text: Venting prevents explo-si-on. Ooh, this is hard. Where's my Tab?
jokes.setup: Homer reading important nuclear safety warning
jokes.punchline: Homer finding life-or-death decision difficult and getting distracted by beverage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's poor priorities with Tab callback

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... donuts.

jokes.text: Aw, no! The corn! Paul Newman's gonna have my legs broke.
jokes.setup: Nuclear gas damages crops
jokes.punchline: Farmer's specific fear of Paul Newman violence over corn
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with unexpected Paul Newman reference

dialogue.speaker: Farmer
dialogue.line: My crops are destroyed! The nuclear gas from the plant has ruined everything!

dialogue.speaker: Farmer
dialogue.line: But you know what I'm really worried about? Paul Newman showing up and beating me senseless over my corn.

jokes.text: When I grow up, I wanna be a lardo on workmen's comp... just like Dad.
jokes.setup: Bart commenting on school vs. staying home
jokes.punchline: Bart's twisted career aspiration to be disabled like Homer
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect Bart voice and social commentary

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Man, school sucks. I'd rather stay home.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Bart, you need an education.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Why? Look at Dad. He's got a good job at the plant and he's completely incompetent.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: When I grow up, I wanna be just like Dad—disabled and stupid.

jokes.text: I wash myself with a rag on a stick.
jokes.setup: Homer demonstrating his daily routine at extreme weight
jokes.punchline: Absurd hygiene method due to inability to reach himself
jokes.explanation: Iconic visual gag with perfect economy - became widely referenced and memed

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I use a long stick to scrub my back. And for my front, I just... rub against the fence like a bear.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's disgusting!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, it works!

jokes.text: Remember when I giggled at his Sherlock Holmes hat? He sulked for a week and then closed his detective agency.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining Homer's sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Absurd backstory about Homer having a detective agency over hat mockery
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist backstory with perfect character specificity

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer's very sensitive about his appearance. When he was younger, he had a detective agency, but it fell apart when someone made fun of his hat.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It was a very nice hat!

jokes.text: Pro: I'm drought and famine resistant.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his weight gain in pros/cons discussion
jokes.punchline: Treating obesity as survival advantage in apocalyptic terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with perfect economy and unexpected evolutionary advantage angle

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: When society collapses and there's no food, who's gonna survive? The guy with the six-pack abs or the guy with a six-pack under his shirt?

jokes.text: the slim, lazy Homer you knew is dead. Now I'm a big, fat dynamo!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his transformation to Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer claiming increased productivity while getting fatter and lazier
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic character moment with perfect self-delusion

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, I'm not getting fatter and lazier. I'm just more aerodynamic. See, the extra weight is actually helping me move faster because I'm more streamlined.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that makes no sense.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And another thing - I've been incredibly productive. Why, just yesterday I moved the couch three inches closer to the fridge. That's efficiency!

jokes.text: Hey, Miss Doesn't Find Me Attractive Sexually Anymore. I just tripled my productivity.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing Marge while working
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive nickname while claiming improved work performance
jokes.explanation: Good passive-aggressive humor typical of Homer's relationship dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, I'm working harder than ever now. In fact, I'm so productive, I've started calling myself 'The Machine.'

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's wonderful, Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, 'The Machine' also breaks down a lot and leaks fluid.

jokes.text: All I have to type is 'Y.' So many letters to choose from. I'll pick 'Y.' 'Y.' 'Y.'
jokes.setup: Homer discovering pattern in computer responses
jokes.punchline: Homer treating single letter choice as complex decision with variety
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's simple-minded approach to work

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, a computer that understands me!

dialogue.speaker: Computer
dialogue.line: Would you like A) food, B) beer, or C) food?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Ooh, so many choices!

jokes.text: Mmm! I can feel three kinds of softness.
jokes.setup: Homer eating fabric softener sample
jokes.punchline: Homer analyzing non-food item like food critic
jokes.explanation: Good absurd eating humor with Homer's food obsession extending to inedible items

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... fabric softener. Oh, wait. This isn't food, is it?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, it's too late now. Let me give it a fair review: Smooth, creamy texture. Hint of lavender. A little chalky aftertaste, but not bad overall. I'd give it a solid seven out of ten.

jokes.text: I'll see to it you don't get apricot one.
jokes.setup: TV soap opera dialogue about inheritance
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'apricot' for 'appropriate'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun with little connection to main story

dialogue.speaker: TV Character
dialogue.line: You can't just take the inheritance! That's not apricot!

dialogue.speaker: TV Character
dialogue.line: Well, it's certainly not a plum either.

jokes.text: I heard that guy's ass has its own congressman.
jokes.setup: Kids discussing Homer's size
jokes.punchline: Absurd political representation joke about Homer's size
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist hyperbole with political satire - very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Kid 1
dialogue.line: Wow, your dad is really fat!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, he's so big, he needs his own congressional district.

dialogue.speaker: Kid 2
dialogue.line: Does that mean he gets his own representative?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: No, he IS the representative. And the constituency. And the lobbyists.

jokes.text: Hey, look. He's tryin' to get up to yell at us. Don't make me close that shade!
jokes.setup: Kids mocking Homer through window
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic threat while struggling to stand
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer's limited threat options

dialogue.speaker: Kids
dialogue.line: Ha ha!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well when I get in there, you kids are gonna get it!

dialogue.speaker: Kids
dialogue.line: Ha ha!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm gonna... I'm gonna... ow! My back!

jokes.text: You promised Mom you wouldn't wear your dress outside. Nuts to that. I'm goin' to the movies.
jokes.setup: Lisa reminding Homer of his agreement with Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's defiant rejection while wearing muumuu in public
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's rebellious streak

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, you promised Mom you wouldn't wear that muumuu in public anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't care what I promised! This thing is comfortable!

jokes.text: You promised Mom you wouldn't wear your dress outside. Nuts to that. I'm goin' to the movies
jokes.setup: Bart reminding Homer of his promise
jokes.punchline: Homer's defiance about wearing the muumuu publicly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's stubborn defiance of social norms

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, you promised you'd wear the muumuu to the store.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not wearing a dress in public, boy. I don't care what I promised.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But Dad...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No! The answer is no! I'd rather die than let people see me in a muumuu.

jokes.text: Hey, fatty.! I got a movie for ya. A Fridge Too Far.
jokes.setup: Person mocking Homer at movie theater
jokes.punchline: Pun on war movie 'A Bridge Too Far' with food reference
jokes.explanation: Decent pun with good economy but somewhat predictable fat joke

dialogue.speaker: Heckler
dialogue.line: A Bridge Too Far? More like a Chili Dog Too Far!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey!

jokes.text: The overweight individuals in this country... are just as smart and talented and hardworking as everybody else. And they're gonna make their voices heard. All they need is a leader!
jokes.setup: Homer giving passionate speech about overweight discrimination
jokes.punchline: Homer positioning himself as leader while proving opposite of his claims
jokes.explanation: Good ironic character moment with Homer's misplaced righteousness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We are not a disease! We are not a burden! We are Americans!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And for too long, we have been denied the most basic human right — the right to be fat!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I stand before you today as your leader.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A man of discipline, of restraint, of impeccable willpower.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: *stuffs donut in mouth*

jokes.text: What happened to my bird?
jokes.setup: Homer returns home after inspiring speech
jokes.punchline: Discovering he accidentally killed his bird through neglect
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy puncturing Homer's heroic moment with reality of his irresponsibility

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm home! And I've never felt more alive! I gave the most inspiring speech of my life!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's wonderful, Homer. By the way, have you fed Bart's parakeet?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Parakeet? I thought that was a decorative pillow.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad! No!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh... oh no. Well, I guess my inspiring speech inspired the bird to... not live.

jokes.text: The fingers you have used to dial are too fat. To obtain a special dialing wand... please mash the keypad with your palm now.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to call nuclear plant in emergency
jokes.punchline: Automated system with specific message for fat finger problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke that became iconic - perfect premise execution and timing

dialogue.speaker: Phone System
dialogue.line: If you are calling about a fat finger problem, press 1.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: *presses 1*

dialogue.speaker: Phone System
dialogue.line: We're sorry, but there is no escape from this menu.

jokes.text: You gotta slow your car down and let me in because I'm a big fat guy and I can't go anywhere. Because there's gonna be some poison gas.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately trying to get ride to plant
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic self-description followed by understated crisis description
jokes.explanation: Good character desperation with typical Homer priorities

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not a smart man, I don't know much about science, a potato flew around my room before you came in here.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Jim, what did I say to you?

jokes.text: Ah! Oh! That's raspberry.
jokes.setup: Homer stuck in playground equipment during emergency
jokes.punchline: Homer identifying flavor while trapped
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's food obsession even in crisis

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... strawberry.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: We're gonna need the Jaws of Life!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And hurry! This painted metal is making me thirsty!

jokes.text: Stupid switch! I wish I had my reaching broom.
jokes.setup: Homer unable to reach emergency shut-off switch
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical solution recognition while trapped
jokes.explanation: Good character moment referencing his adaptive tools for obesity

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why do they always put these emergency shut-off switches so high up?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, wait. I could just use the ladder.

jokes.text: Homer, your bravery and quick thinking... have turned a potential Chernobyl... into a mere Three Mile Island. Bravo.
jokes.setup: Burns praising Homer for averting disaster
jokes.punchline: Burns treating Three Mile Island as acceptable nuclear accident outcome
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect Burns character voice and nuclear disaster hierarchy

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Excellent work, Simpson. You've prevented a catastrophe.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You know, Three Mile Island wasn't so bad. Only a few thousand deaths, minimal property damage. We could've done worse.

jokes.text: I think it's ironic that Dad saved the day... while a slimmer man would have fallen to his death.
jokes.setup: Lisa reflecting on Homer's heroics
jokes.punchline: Noting that Homer's obesity was actually advantageous
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation that validates Homer's earlier 'drought and famine resistant' joke

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: You know, Dad's obesity, which we've always seen as a character flaw, actually turned out to be an advantage. All that extra mass gave him the momentum to push through the wall.

jokes.text: And I think it's ironic that, for once, Dad's butt... prevented the release of toxic gas
jokes.setup: Bart adding to Lisa's ironic observation
jokes.punchline: Ironic reversal of Homer's usual gas production problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy with perfect Bart-style crude humor building on Lisa's point

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, I think you're lactose intolerant.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, and he's been gassy all day.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not lactose intolerant! I'm just... producing methane at an alarming rate.

jokes.text: One. One. One! Bah! I'll just pay for the blasted liposuction.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to do one pushup as part of Homer's return to health
jokes.punchline: Burns giving up immediately and choosing expensive medical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - wealthy man's solution to simple problems

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: One... two... three...

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, schedule me for expensive experimental surgery.

tmdb_overview: Homer tries to gain sixty pounds so he can stay home on disability. When he nearly causes a catastrophe at the plant, Homer reconsiders his plan.
insight: Homer's 69-joke gauntlet hits 3.7 per minute: Season 7's density peak fueled by weight-gain obsession.
